body.title: ToolStats._samples should be newest-N, not sorted-N
body.result: Shipped in e6b0539d. Replaced bisect.insort + pop(0) pattern (which evicted fastest, not oldest) with collections.deque(maxlen=1000) for insertion-order eviction. Percentile math sorts a snapshot on demand. Regression test: 500 slow + 1500 fast samples → p50/p95 = 10 (reflects newest-1000 fast tail, not sorted-top-1000 slow bias). 18 tool_registry tests pass.
body.description:
Pass-2 Area 5 Gap 2 (SHIP). `ToolStats` in lab/utility_closet/registry.py uses `bisect.insort` to maintain sorted order then `pop(0)` when full — this drops the fastest call, not the oldest. Over 1000+ calls with mixed latencies, kept set skews toward slow; p50/p95 biased high. Fix: use collections.deque(maxlen=1000) for insertion-order + fixed capacity, compute percentiles on demand from a sorted copy.

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/registry.py (ToolStats), tests/test_tool_registry.py (if exists).
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — ToolStats internals + percentile math. OUT — altering Tool interface, altering ToolRegistry API, changing sample cap from 1000.
**Test plan:** record 2000 samples of known distribution (alternating slow/fast); assert p50/p95 reflect the last-1000 window rather than the sorted-top-1000 (pre-fix, newest-slow sample would push p50 up even though half the recent calls are fast).
